'''Eribe Doro''' (born 26 March 2001) is a professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} for the [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[Super League]].

==Background==
Doro is of Nigerian descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://saddind.co.uk/eribe-has-chance-to-play-rugby-league-for-nigeria/ |title=Eribe has chance to play rugby league for Nigeria |first=Gary |last=Carter |publisher=Saddleworth Independent |date=21 August 2019}}</ref> He went to [[The Blue Coat School, Oldham]].

Doro played for [[Saddleworth Rangers]] as a junior before signing with the [[Warrington Wolves]] academy.<ref name="KR">{{cite web |title=Doro joins Robins on two-year deal from 2025 |date=26 June 2024 |publisher=Hull KR|url=https://hullkr.co.uk/news/doro-joins-robins-on-two-year-deal-from-2025 |access-date=2024-10-14}}</ref>

==Career==
===Warrington Wolves===
Doro made his [[Super League]] début in round 12 of the [[Super League XXV results|2020 Super League season]], for the [[Warrington Wolves]] against the [[Castleford Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/castleford-vs-warrington/teams/54475 |title=Starting Lineups - Castleford vs Warrington|publisher=Sky Sports|date=10 September 2020}}</ref>

In round 11 of the [[2021 Super League season]], he scored his first try in the Super League during Warrington's 44-18 victory over [[Leigh Leopards|Leigh]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12204/12341187/warrington-wolves-steve-price-praises-gareth-widdops-performance-after-missing-england-selection/|title=Warrington Wolves' Steve Price praises Gareth Widdop's performance after missing England selection|website=www.skysports.com}}</ref>

====Widnes Vikings (loan)====
Doro was loaned to [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[RFL Championship]] for the 2021 season, this was extended for the 2022 season without a call back option.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://widnesvikings.co.uk/eribe-doro-to-remain-at-widnes-for-2022-season/ |title=Eribe Doro to remain at Widnes for 2022 season |website=Widnes Vikings |date=21 February 2022 |access-date=13 May 2026}}</ref>

===Halifax Panthers===
Doro signed for [[Halifax Panthers]] in the [[RFL Championship]] for the 2023 season.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://halifaxpanthers.co.uk/panthers-news/the-mask-of-doro-eribe-signs-for-2023/ |title=The Mask of Doro - Eribe signs for 2023! |website=Halifax Panthers |date=10 November 2022 |access-date=13 May 2026}}</ref>

===Bradford Bulls===
On 3 August 2023, Doro transferred to [[Bradford Bulls]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Telegraph & Argus|date=3 Aug 2023|accessdate=4 Dec 2023|title=Bradford Bulls confirm forward Eribe Doro as signing|url=https://www.thetelegraphandargus.co.uk/sport/sportlatest/23699750.bradford-bulls-confirm-forward-eribe-doro-signing/}}</ref>

===Hull Kingston Rovers===
Ahead of the 2025 season, on 27 June 2024, Doro signed for [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=27 Jun 2024|accessdate=3 Oct 2024|title=Hull KR sign Bradford prop Doro for 2025|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4nge07rl4do}}</ref><ref name="KR" /> Doro was named in the club's match day squad for the [[2025 Challenge Cup final]], and was on the bench as the 18th man.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://hullkr.co.uk/news/21-man-squad-for-the-betfred-challenge-cup-is-in |title=21-man squad for the Betfred Challenge Cup is in |website=Hull KR |access-date=13 May 2026}}</ref> KR won the game 8-6.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/articles/cn4qdpnq32wo |title=Hull KR beat Warrington to end 45-year Challenge Cup title wait |work=BBC Sport |date=7 June 2025 |access-date=13 May 2026}}</ref>

====Bradford Bulls (loan)====
During the 2025 season, Doro spent two two-week loans at [[Bradford Bulls|Bradford]], starting 17 April<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=17 Apr 2025|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Promising Hull KR duo make Championship moves as loans locked in|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/promising-hull-kr-duo-make-championship-moves-as-loans-locked-in}}</ref> and 2 July respectively.<ref>{{cite web|website=Bradford Bulls|date=2 Jul 2025|access-date=7 Jul 2025|title=REEBS REJOINS ON LOAN!|url=https://www.bradfordbulls.co.uk/article/3163/reebs-rejoins-on-loan}}</ref>

===Return to Bradford Bulls===
On 28 October 2025, Doro returned to Bradford on permanent bases, signing a two year deal for the recently promoted [[Super League]] club.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=28 Oct 2025|title=Bradford Bulls re-sign prop Doro|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/cvgknezp53po}}</ref>
